concept: Assistive / Supporting a Chosen Function or Access Need
description: Designed or used to support a person's chosen activity, function, or access requirement. A device, communication practice, or arrangement may be assistive in one context and ordinary in another; 'naphe' (help) names an act of aid, while 'naphelu' marks the supporting role something has.
sound_symbolism: Dental 'na' opens broadly with a steady nasal resonance. Stressed 'phe' brings unvoiced breath to the lips and repeats the second half of 'naphe' (help). Final 'lu' releases the tongue into deep 'u'. The complete sound of 'naphe' leads into a low liquid continuation.
grammatical_notes: A predicative or attributive adjective: 'ha keli naphelu nai.' (This device is assistive.) It commonly modifies 'keli' (device), 'norae' (method), or 'phelotu' (format). The quality-noun rule supplies assistiveness when the property itself is discussed.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A tool earns the description through the function and access it supports, whether it is handmade, digital, shared, or personal.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Calling a device assistive describes its role without making its user passive or granting anyone authority over its use.

tags.activity: supporting a chosen function or action
tags.creation: a device or practice shaped for an access purpose
tags.physical: support for bodily or sensory access
